events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer as raw JSON. Need assess schedule. Let's parse image carefully.

Schedule early-start bars, durations, predecessor lists. Activities:
A Mobilize & layout [3d] predecessor none (maybe -)
B Underground rough-in [4d] pred A
C Slab pour [3d] pred B
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C (hatched cure lag cannot compress)
D Framing [5d] pred CC
E Roof dry-in [3d] pred D
F Electrical rough [5d] pred E
G Plumbing top-out [3d] pred E
H HVAC rough [3d] pred E
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H (red inspection hold)
J Insulation [4d] pred I
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J
L Drywall hang [5d] pred K
M Tape & finish [6d] pred L
N Paint [1d] pred M
O Trim carpentry [3d] pred N
P Flooring [4d] pred N
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P

Disruption: Activity H (HVAC rough) needs 1 extra workday — rework discovered at handoff.

Need compute project finish delay in workdays. Since early-start schedule. H has duration 3d originally on critical? Let's determine critical path. Activities F, G, H run parallel after E. F 5d, G 3d, H 3d. I starts when all F,G,H finish. Thus F is longest (5d) and drives I start. H has float relative to I? H finish must be by day start of I. E ends at? Let's compute dates.

Assume start at day 0. A: 0-3. B:3-7. C:7-10. CC:10-13 (3d cure). D:13-18. E:18-21. F:21-26. G:21-24. H:21-24. I:26-27. J:27-31. K:31-32. L:32-37. M:37-43. N:43-44. O:44-47. P:44-48. Q:48-49. Project finish 49? If starts at 0 and bars represent workdays. Q final inspection at ~48-49. So baseline finish day 49 (or maybe 49 workdays). H is 21-24; F is 21-26. I at 26. H has 2 days float (H finishes day 24 vs I day 26). Actually H duration 3, F 5, G 3. H free float 2? G finishes 24 also, F 26. So H can slip 2 days without delaying I. A 1-day extra workday for H makes H duration 4, finish day 25, still before F finish day 26. I unchanged, project finish unchanged. So value = 0. Completion date does not slip. Driver: H has float because Electrical rough (F) is the longest path to rough inspection; H and G are not critical.

Need JSON matching schema: decision: pass (not fail because schedule impact none? It asks decide work can proceed/blocked; H rework discovered but delay absorbed; maybe decision pass? Or maybe needs_more_info? We can assess as pass because schedule remains valid, no dangerous non-compliance. risk: low. work_stage: in-progress (given). component_conditions: empty. percent_complete: estimate overall progress? Need numeric estimate of overall progress. In the schedule image, it shows lookahead schedule, not actual progress bars. The stage is in-progress. But percent_complete maybe estimate based on schedule? We have no actual progress data. Could set maybe 0? But work_stage in-progress, but schedule bars are planned lookahead. Since observed in-progress; but image is schedule not physical. We might set percent_complete to estimated overall project completion maybe around 0? Hmm. The schema percent_complete numeric estimate of overall progress. If we cannot determine, maybe 0 or null? It expects number. Use 0? But maybe better to estimate based on current schedule position? Since disruption at H handoff means H was underway. But we don't know actual progress. Could set 30? Need avoid hallucination. Maybe 0 because schedule assessment not physical. But work_stage is in-progress; maybe percent_complete unknown, set 0. We'll set 0.

value 0. sound_source empty? Use "". event_time 0.0. rate 0.0. order [].
workable: list of activity IDs that can still start. The task asks identify what work can proceed, what blocked, recovery plan valid. But required output includes workable list of activity IDs. Need decide which activities can still start given disruption. Since H delay absorbed, no activities blocked beyond original predecessors. At point of disruption (H rework discovered at handoff), H is ongoing. Workable maybe activities not dependent on H's completion and not yet started. But without knowing current status, we can infer from schedul…
